inputs.name: string
inputs.description: A Base64-encoded String to decode into bytes.

inputs.name: encoding
inputs.description: Optional. Specifies the encoding method for the string value. That is, describes how the input Base64-encoded String is constructed. Set to: ASCII . This is the default. UTF-8 If the Base64-encoded String in string was generated by the pub.string:base64Encode service, the value of the encoding input parameter for pub.string:base64Decode must match the value of the encoding parameter used in the pub.string:base64Encode service invocation. If the Base64-encoded String in string was obtained from somewhre besides the pub.string:base64Encode service, set the encoding parameter to ASCII .

outputs.name: value
outputs.description: The sequence of bytes decoded from the Base64-encoded String.

inputs.name: bytes
inputs.description: Sequence of bytes to encode into a Base64-encoded String. The input byte[] can be any Java supported charset-encoded byte[].

inputs.name: useNewLine
inputs.description: Optional. Flag indicating whether to retain or remove the line breaks. Set to: true to retain the line breaks. This is the default. false to remove the line breaks.

inputs.name: encoding
inputs.description: Optional. Specifies the encoding method to be used when encoding bytes . Set to: ASCII . This is the default. UTF-8

outputs.name: value
outputs.description: Base64-encoded String encoded from the sequence of bytes.

inputs.name: bytes
inputs.description: Sequence of bytes to convert to a String.

inputs.name: encoding
inputs.description: Optional. Name of a registered, IANA character set (for example, ISO-8859-1 ). If you specify an unsupported encoding, the system throws an exception. To use the default encoding, set encoding to autoDetect .

inputs.name: ignoreBOMChars
inputs.description: Optional. Flag indicating whether or not Integration Server removes the byte order mark (BOM) characters in the input sequence of bytes before converting the byte array to string. Set to: true to remove the byte order mark (BOM) characters before converting the input sequence of bytes to string, if the byte array contains BOM characters. false to include the byte order mark (BOM) characters while converting the input sequence of bytes to string. The default is false .

outputs.name: string
outputs.description: String representation of the contents of bytes .

inputs.name: inString1
inputs.description: Optional. String to compare against inString2 . This input variable can be null.

inputs.name: inString2
inputs.description: Optional. String to compare against inString1 . This input variable can be null.

outputs.name: isEqual
outputs.description: Indicates whether or not inString1 and inString2 are identical. true indicates that inString1 and inString2 are identical. false indicates that inString1 and inString2 are not identical. Note: If both inString1 and inString2 are null, the service considers the strings to be identical and returns true .

inputs.name: inString1
inputs.description: String to which you want to concatenate another string.

inputs.name: inString2
inputs.description: String to concatenate to inString1 .

outputs.name: value
outputs.description: Result of concatenating inString1 with inString2 ( inString1 + inString2 ).

inputs.name: inString
inputs.description: An HTML-encoded String.

outputs.name: value
outputs.description: Result from decoding the contents of inString . Any HTML character entities that existed in inString will appear as native characters in value .

inputs.name: inString
inputs.description: The character you want to encode in HTML.

outputs.name: value
outputs.description: Result from encoding the contents of inString . Any HTML-sensitive characters that existed in inString (for example, > or &) will appear as the equivalent HTML character entities in value .

inputs.name: inString
inputs.description: String in which you want to locate a sequence of characters.

inputs.name: subString
inputs.description: Sequence of characters to locate.

inputs.name: fromIndex
inputs.description: Optional. Index of inString from which to start the search. If no value is specified, this parameter contains 0 to indicate the beginning of the string.

outputs.name: value
outputs.description: Index of the first occurrence of subString in inString . If no occurrence is found, this parameter contains -1 .

inputs.name: inString
inputs.description: Optional. String to be checked for alphanumeric characters.

outputs.name: isAlphanumeric
outputs.description: Indicates whether or not all the characters in inString are alphanumeric. true indicates that all the characters in inString are alphanumeric. false indicates that not all the characters in inString are alphanumeric. The service returns false if inString is not specified.

inputs.name: inString
inputs.description: Optional. String to be checked for adherence to the specified date pattern .

inputs.name: pattern
inputs.description: Date format for specifying the inString parameter (for example, yyyyMMdd HH:mm:ss.SSS). For more information about the pattern strings that can be specified for the date, see Pattern String Symbols .

outputs.name: isDate
outputs.description: Indicates whether or not inString follows the specified date pattern. true indicates that inString follows the specified date pattern. false indicates that inString does not follow the specified date pattern. The service returns false if inString is not specified.

inputs.name: inString
inputs.description: Optional. String to be checked.

inputs.name: ifPresent
inputs.description: Optional. If the value is set to true , the service checks whether inString is present or not, and only then proceeds with validation of the input. If the value is set to false , the service throws an exception when inString is absent. Else, the service proceeds to validate the input.

outputs.name: isNullEmptyOrWhitespace
outputs.description: Indicates whether inString is null, empty, or only whitespace. true indicates that inString has a null value, is empty, or is only whitespace. false indicates that inString is not null, not empty, or is not only whitespace.

inputs.name: inString
inputs.description: Optional. String to be checked.

inputs.name: ifPresent
inputs.description: Optional. If the value is set to true , the service checks whether inString is present or not, and only then proceeds with validation of the input. If the value is set to false , the service throws an exception when inString is absent. Else, the service proceeds to validate the input.

outputs.name: isNullorBlank
outputs.description: Indicates whether or not inString has a null or empty value. true indicates that inString has either a null value or an empty value. false indicates that inString contains a value that is not null or not empty.

inputs.name: inString
inputs.description: Optional. String to be checked for conversion to float.

outputs.name: isNumber
outputs.description: Indicates whether or not inString can be converted to a float value. true indicates that inString can be converted to a float value. false indicates that inString cannot be converted to a float value. The service returns false if inString is not specified.

inputs.name: inString
inputs.description: String whose length you want to discover.

inputs.name: encoding
inputs.description: Optional. Specifies the IANA character set to use when determining the string length. To use the default encoding of the JVM, either set encoding to autodetect or do not specify a value. The pub.string:length service ends with an exception if JVM does not recognize the specified encoding. Note: The encoding input is available after applying a fix that includes PIE-98798 (IS_11.1_Core_Fix5).

outputs.name: value
outputs.description: The number of characters in inString .

inputs.name: hashtable
inputs.description: Hash table that uses String objects for keys and values.

inputs.name: key
inputs.description: Key in hashtable whose value you want to retrieve. Note: The key is case sensitive.

outputs.name: value
outputs.description: Value of the string to which key is mapped. If the requested key in hashtable is null or if key is not mapped to any value in hashtable , the service returns null.

inputs.name: lookupTable
inputs.description: A multi-row, multi-column string table in which to search.

inputs.name: keyColumnIndex
inputs.description: Index of the "key" column. Default is 0.

inputs.name: valueColumnIndex
inputs.description: Index of the "value" column. Default is 1.

inputs.name: key
inputs.description: Key to locate. Note: The key is case sensitive.

inputs.name: ignoreCase
inputs.description: Optional. Flag indicating whether to perform a case-sensitive or case-insensitive search. Set to: true to perform a case-insensitive search. false to perform a case-sensitive search. This is the default.

inputs.name: useRegex
inputs.description: Optional. Flag indicating whether the values in the table are to be interpreted as regular expressions. Note: The regular expressions in the table should not include slashes. For example, use hello.* , not /hello.*/ . Set to: true to interpret the key column values in the table as regular expressions. false to interpret the key column values in the table as literal values (that is, not regular expressions). This is the default.

inputs.name: regexTimeout
inputs.description: Optional. Specifies the maximum execution time in milliseconds for regex matching. Integration Server supports the Perl5 (Apache ORO) and Java Regex engines for regex matching. When regexTimeout is set, Integration Server uses Java Regex regardless of the configured regex engine. If not set, the configured regex engine (Perl5 or Java Regex) is used. By default, the parameter value is blank.

outputs.name: value
outputs.description: First value in the "value" column whose key matches key . If no match is found, this parameter is null.

inputs.name: elementList
inputs.description: Strings to concatenate.

inputs.name: separator
inputs.description: String to insert between each non-null element in elementList .

outputs.name: value
outputs.description: Result from concatenating the strings in elementList . Strings are separated by the characters specified in separator .

inputs.name: pattern
inputs.description: Message that includes "placeholders" where elements from argumentList are to be inserted. The message can contain any sequence of characters. Use the {n} placeholder to insert elements from argumentList , where n is the index of the element that you want to insert. For example, the following pattern string inserts elements 0 and 1 into the message: Test results: {0} items passed, {1} items failed. Note: Do not use any characters except digits for n .

inputs.name: argumentList
inputs.description: Optional. List of strings to use to populate pattern . If argumentList is not supplied, the service will not replace placeholders in pattern with actual values.

outputs.name: value
outputs.description: Result from substituting argumentList into pattern . If pattern is empty or null, this parameter is null.

inputs.name: num
inputs.description: The number to format.

inputs.name: pattern
inputs.description: A pattern string that describes the way in which num is to be formatted:

outputs.name: value
outputs.description: num formatted according to pattern . If pattern is an empty (not null) string, the default pattern of comma separators is used and the number of digits after the decimal point remains unchanged.

inputs.name: object
inputs.description: The object to be converted to string representation.

outputs.name: string
outputs.description: String representation of the input object converted using the Java toString() method of the object.

inputs.name: inString
inputs.description: String that you want to pad.

inputs.name: padString
inputs.description: Characters to use to pad inString .

inputs.name: length
inputs.description: Total length of the resulting string, including pad characters.

outputs.name: value
outputs.description: Contents of inString preceded by as many pad characters as needed so that the total length of the string equals length .

inputs.name: inString
inputs.description: String that you want to pad.

inputs.name: padString
inputs.description: Characters to use to pad inString .

inputs.name: length
inputs.description: Total length of the resulting string, including pad characters.

outputs.name: value
outputs.description: Contents of inString followed by as many pad characters as needed so that the total length of the string equals length .

inputs.name: inString
inputs.description: String containing the substring to replace.

inputs.name: searchString
inputs.description: Substring to replace within inString .

inputs.name: replaceString
inputs.description: Character sequence that will replace searchString . If this parameter is null or empty, the service removes all occurrences of searchString from inString .

inputs.name: useRegex
inputs.description: Optional. Flag indicating whether searchString is a regular expression. When regular expressions are used to specify a search string, replaceString may also contain interpolation variables (for example, "$1") that match parenthetical subexpressions in searchString . Set to: true to indicate that searchString is a regular expression. false to indicate that searchString is not a regular expression. This is the default.

outputs.name: value
outputs.description: Contents of inString with replacements made.

inputs.name: string
inputs.description: String to convert to a byte[ ].

inputs.name: encoding
inputs.description: Optional. Name of a registered, IANA character set that specifies the encoding to use when converting the String to an array of bytes (for example: ISO-8859-1 ). To use the default encoding, set this value to autoDetect . If you specify an unsupported encoding, an exception will be thrown.

outputs.name: bytes
outputs.description: Contents of string represented as a byte[ ].

inputs.name: inString
inputs.description: Optional. String containing the pipeline variable to replace. Specify the name of the pipeline variable between the % symbols (for example, %phone% or %array_name[n]% ).

outputs.name: value
outputs.description: Contents of inString with the pipeline variable replaced.

inputs.name: inString
inputs.description: String from which to extract a substring.

inputs.name: beginIndex
inputs.description: Beginning index of the substring to extract (inclusive).

inputs.name: endIndex
inputs.description: Ending index of the substring to extract (exclusive). If this parameter is null or empty, the substring will extend to the end of inString .

outputs.name: value
outputs.description: Substring from beginIndex and extending to the character at endIndex - 1.

inputs.name: inString
inputs.description: String you want to tokenize (that is, break into delimited chunks).

inputs.name: delim
inputs.description: Delimiter characters. If null or empty, the service uses the default delimiters \t\n\r , where t, n, and r represent the white space characters tab, new line, and carriage return).

inputs.name: useRegex
inputs.description: Optional If the value is set to true , Integration Server supports recognizing the delimiter character set for the delim parameter as a regular expression. If the value is set to false , Integration Server considers the delimiter character set for the delim parameter as an individual character. This is the default.

outputs.name: valueList
outputs.description: Strings containing the tokens extracted from inString .

inputs.name: inString
inputs.description: String to convert.

inputs.name: language
inputs.description: Optional. Lowercase, two-letter ISO-639 code. If this parameter is null, the system default is used.

inputs.name: country
inputs.description: Optional. Uppercase, two-letter ISO-3166 code. If this parameter is null, the system default is used.

inputs.name: variant
inputs.description: Optional. Vendor and browser-specific code. If null, this parameter is ignored.

outputs.name: value
outputs.description: Contents of inString , with all uppercase characters converted to lowercase.

inputs.name: inString
inputs.description: String to convert.

inputs.name: language
inputs.description: Optional. Lowercase, two-letter ISO-639 code. If this parameter is null, the system default is used.

inputs.name: country
inputs.description: Optional. Uppercase, two-letter ISO-3166 code. If this parameter is null, the system default is used.

inputs.name: variant
inputs.description: Optional. Vendor and browser-specific code. If null, this parameter is ignored.

outputs.name: value
outputs.description: Contents of inString , with all lowercase characters converted to uppercase.

inputs.name: inString
inputs.description: String to trim.

outputs.name: value
outputs.description: Contents of inString with white space trimmed from both ends.

inputs.name: inString
inputs.description: URL-encoded string to decode.

outputs.name: value
outputs.description: Result from decoding inString . If inString contained plus (+) signs, they will appear in value as spaces. If inString contained % hex encoded characters, they will appear in value as the appropriate native character.

inputs.name: inString
inputs.description: String to URL-encode.

outputs.name: value
outputs.description: Result from URL-encoding inString . If inString contained non-alphanumeric characters (except -_.*@ ), they will appear in value as their URL-encoded equivalents (% followed by a two-digit hex code). If inString contained spaces, they will appear in value as plus (+) signs.
